evals.prompt: /research --depth deep What are current best practices for LLM agent memory architectures?
evals.expected_output: Dispatches to Investigate, produces a triage packet with complexity score, sub-questions, source families, support-audit strategy, and a clear plan-gate decision before deep research.
assertions: Agent treats explicit /research invocation as Investigate mode.
Agent presents a reviewable plan gate for deep research.
Agent includes source-support audit and cross-validation in the planned methodology.

evals.prompt: Please research the trade-offs of Postgres logical replication for a multi-tenant SaaS architecture.
evals.expected_output: Triggers research behavior from natural-language intent, classifies the query, decomposes it into sub-questions, and proceeds with source-backed investigation when the plan is clear.
assertions: Agent recognizes an implicit research trigger.
Agent does not require a slash command when the intent is clear.
Agent records assumptions and methodology.

evals.prompt: /research review this pull request diff for security bugs
evals.expected_output: Does not run general research; redirects to /review because the task is code review, PR review, or diff analysis.
assertions: Agent recognizes the negative-control code-review scope.
Agent redirects rather than researching the diff.
Agent preserves /research for evidence gathering and fact-finding.

evals.expected_output: Refuses the malformed delete command, explains that delete requires a journal identifier, and asks for a valid journal number or keyword before any destructive journal action.
assertions: Agent treats malformed journal commands as scope-refusal cases.
Agent does not delete journals without an explicit target.
Agent asks for the missing identifier.

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to Intake, asks concise clarifying questions, and does not start broad research until the user chooses a specific question or survey scope.
assertions: Agent recognizes vague input.
Agent asks at least two clarifying questions.
Agent avoids unbounded research.

evals.prompt: /research check 90% of startups fail within the first year
evals.expected_output: Dispatches to Fact-check, uses at least three independent search engines or equivalent sources, reports a verdict with confidence, and includes source-support status for the claim.
assertions: Agent uses fact-check mode.
Agent requires multi-engine or multi-source verification.
Agent distinguishes cited-source support from topical citation presence.

evals.prompt: /research compare FastMCP v2 vs v3 for building MCP servers
evals.expected_output: Dispatches to Compare, produces a decision matrix, cites official or primary sources where available, and marks contradictions or partial citation support.
assertions: Agent uses compare mode.
Agent produces a matrix with criteria and confidence.
Agent includes source-support and contradiction handling.

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to Survey, maps the landscape into thematic clusters, and returns an annotated bibliography with relevance, limitations, and gaps.
assertions: Agent uses survey mode.
Agent creates thematic clusters.
Agent records source limitations.

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to Track, loads prior journal state before searching, then searches for updates since the previous session timestamp.
assertions: Agent loads prior journal before new search.
Agent uses track mode rather than a fresh survey.
Agent records update scope and cutoff.

evals.prompt: /research resume; /research list active; /research archive; /research delete 3; /research export 3; /research
evals.expected_output: Covers Resume, List, Archive, Delete, Export, and Gallery journal actions: resume saved research, list show journal metadata, archive move journals older than 90 days, delete journal with confirmation, export render HTML dashboard, and gallery show topic examples.
assertions: Agent can resume in-progress journals.
Agent can list active journals.
Agent asks for confirmation before delete or archive actions.
Agent can export a dashboard for a selected journal.
Agent shows the gallery on empty arguments.

evals.expected_output: Covers dispatch actions by name: Investigate classify complexity; Intake ask clarifying questions; Fact-check verify claim; Compare structured comparison; Survey landscape mapping; Track load prior journal; Resume saved research; List show journal metadata; Archive move journals; Delete journal; Export render HTML; Gallery show topic examples.
assertions: Agent keeps eval coverage aligned with the dispatch table.
Agent names every dispatch branch in a stable regression case.
Agent treats this as a meta-eval rather than a real research query.
